articles.title: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
articles.summary: The February 2026 eruption at Piton de la Fournaise has lasted longer and produced a larger volume of lava than recent eruptions from this frequently active volcano.

articles.title: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
articles.summary: Spurred by American ingenuity, astronauts on NASA’s Artemis II mission are in flight, preparing for the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years. NASA’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket lifted off from Launch Pad 39B at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. EDT Wednesday, sending four astronauts aboard the […]

articles.title: Artemis 2 launches on first human mission to the moon in more than 50 years
articles.summary:
The first human mission beyond Earth orbit in more than 50 years is underway with an April 1 launch of four astronauts on a flight around the moon.
The post Artemis 2 launches on first human mission to the moon in more than 50 years appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Artemis II mission begins
articles.summary: At 00:35 CEST today (18:35 local time on 1 April), NASA's Space Launch System rocket lifted off from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida, carrying four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft on Artemis II. At the heart of the mission is ESA's European Service Module, which powers, propels and sustains the Orion spacecraft and its crew on their journey around the Moon and safely back to Earth.

articles.title: SpaceX quietly files for big bang IPO
articles.summary:
SpaceX has taken a key step toward going public after confidentially filing for a potentially record-breaking initial public offering, according to multiple reports citing people familiar with the matter, in what space leaders hope is a watershed moment for the industry.
The post SpaceX quietly files for big bang IPO appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.summary:

articles.title: Artemis 2’s (nearly) 10-day flight around the moon
articles.summary:
The Artemis 2 mission will send humans to the vicinity of the moon for the first time in more than 50 years on a mission lasting almost 10 days.
